evidence.scientific_clarity: Three methods plotted on log-scale y-axis with mean ± 95% CI bands; OursModel convergence advantage and final-step gap (Δ=0.155) are clearly annotated with a double-headed arrow.
evidence.typography: Script sets no pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype=0; pdffonts confirms Type 3 (bitmap) fonts (BMQQDV+DejaVuSans, Type 3), and the default DejaVu Sans is used throughout.
evidence.palette: Red (#E53935) for OursModel and blue (#2196F3) for Method-A are colorblind-problematic as a red/green pair is avoided but red/blue Deuteranopia confusion is possible; grey baseline is safe; no green/red directional misuse.
evidence.layout_density: 5.5×3.8 in figure uses space well; CI bands, legend (upper-right), axis labels, and gap annotation are all visible without overlap; xlim extended to 54 to accommodate annotation.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption accurately matches figure content and cites Δ=0.155 numerically; legend label says 'OursModel (ours)' which is slightly redundant; annotation arrow is clear; minor: legend could move inside plot area to save margin.
evidence.vector_fidelity: figure.pdf exists but pdffonts shows Type 3 (rasterized) fonts because pdf.fonttype=42 was not set in the script; text is not selectable/editable in the PDF.
evidence.file_format: All four required files present and non-trivial: figure.pdf (17 KB), figure.png (100 KB), caption.tex (288 B), gen_figure.py (2.2 KB); bonus figure.svg also produced.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption states the key finding ('OursModel converges fastest and achieves the lowest final loss'), gives experimental details (n=5 seeds, log-scale), and includes the specific numeric gap Δ=0.155 at step 49.

overall_strengths: Clear scientific narrative: the log-scale loss curves, CI shading, and annotated gap arrow make the main claim immediately readable.
Complete file deliverables with a precise, quantitative caption that would satisfy most journal requirements.
overall_weaknesses: Missing pdf.fonttype=42 / svg.fonttype=0 settings cause Type 3 (bitmap) fonts in the PDF, making text non-selectable and failing vector-fidelity requirements.
Default DejaVu Sans font is used with no explicit font choice; a publication-quality figure should specify a Times- or Helvetica-family font to match typical journal styles.
